name: Alice Stewart
subject: #CruzCrew Daily Briefing
disclaimer_text:
PAID FOR
BY CRUZ FOR PRESIDENT
body:
Dear Friend,

Today Ted Cruz is
hosting a roundtable with Heidi, his mother Eleanor, Carly
Fiorina, and Wisconsin Family Action President Julaine Appling.
He will be talking to them about the issues that matter to
mothers, working women, and our children.

Ted and Heidi have
two young daughters, Carolina and Catherine. They are working
tirelessly on the campaign trail each day because they care about
their future - and the future of kids across the country. Yet, in
the Obama economy, women have been hurt by the President's
job-crushing policies: the percentage of women in the work force
is now the lowest it has been in more than 25 years.

Ted understands that
women like his mom, Eleanor, who was the first in her family to
go to college, had the opportunities they did because they worked
hard to achieve what others said was impossible. And he also
understands that if we want to make sure that all our daughters
and sons have that same opportunity, we need to remove the
government barriers to prosperity and make it easier -- not more
difficult -- to get a job, provide for our families, and have a
better future than their parents and grandparents.

Best,

Alice Stewart
Communications
Director

From: Scott Walker
Date: Mon, Mar 28, 2016
Subject: Endorsement
Friend,
It will probably be no surprise to you that President Obama has it wrong again.
America's #1 Senate race is in Wisconsin where three-term liberal Senator Russ Feingold is waging a vicious rematch against my friend Ron Johnson, who unseated him in 2010.
**_And President Obama has endorsed Senator Russ Feingold,_** calling him "honest," "independent-minded," and "not driven by what's politically expedient." Going 0 for 3, President Obama is wrong on all counts.
**_We know where President Obama stands, but what I really want to know is where you stand, Friend._**
As one of our nation's most committed conservatives, **_I hope you will make a generous contribution to my friend Ron Johnson's campaign of $10, $35, $50, $100, $250, or more today._**
Russ Feingold and Barack Obama voted the same way when they were in the Senate together from 2005-2009. And Senator Feingold was an early supporter of "Obama for President."
In the "You Scratch My Back and I'll Scratch Yours" world of Washington, it is business as usual. And it is a mutual admiration society.
Ron Johnson is everything President Obama and Senator Feingold are not:
* He is a conservative champion and a strict constitutionalist.

* He is a citizen-lawmaker who started and grew his own business.

* He wants to **_shake up the Washington status quo_** with sweeping conservative change.

**_With our Republican majority hanging by a thread -- and an uncertain presidential contest -- this is the race in which _your prompt and generous contribution WILL make a difference.__**
Please be as generous as you can today, Friend, and thanks for everything you do.
Governor Scott Walker

name: Corry Bliss
subject: Tough on China
body:
| Peter -- Ted Strickland talks tough on China, but like on so many issues, his actions don't match his words. Today, we're releasing a new web video called "Tough on China." You can be one of the first to see it by [clicking here](http://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DxntdI8O0WH8%26feature%3Dyoutu.be&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=manipulation&n=1).

[](http://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DxntdI8O0WH8%26feature%3Dyoutu.be&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=manipulation&n=2)
The truth is Rob Portman is the only candidate in this race fighting China \-- standing up to his own party to stop China's currency manipulation and protecting Ohio jobs when China cheats.

But in Congress, Ted Strickland refused to vote to hold China accountable for unfair trade practices. As governor, despite his strong rhetoric, his administration opened a trade office in China and he even gave a $4 million taxpayer-funded loan to a company despite knowing the firm was building a Chinese assembly plant.

While Ted Strickland and his liberal allies talk tough, Rob has the record of fighting for Ohio workers. Ohio simply can't afford a return to Ted Strickland's Ohio when the state lost more than 350,000 jobs and ranked 48th in job creation.

Please watch our video and help us make sure Ohio voters see "Tough on China" by donating today. [](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/keep_our_ad_online?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=manipulation&n=3)

[
](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/keep_our_ad_online?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=manipulation&n=7)Sincerely, Corry Bliss [](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/keep_our_ad_online?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=manipulation&n=8) [](http://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=manipulation&n=9)[](http://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=manipulation&n=10)

subject: Two challenges

body:
Fighting two fronts | Friend --

We’re heading into one of the toughest stretches of the campaign: We’re fighting on two fronts as Senator Sanders tries to make a comeback and Donald Trump smears our team (not to mention his Republican opponents, immigrants, Muslims, women, our NATO allies, and much of the rest of the planet). I know we can take on both challenges -- but only if we have the resources to make sure voters know what we’re working toward.

That’s why our fundraising deadline tomorrow is so critical. We need to be strong enough to overpower any obstacles that come our way.

body:
I'm sorry to email this late, but I need to know...can you do something for me in the next 20 minutes? | Friend,

I'm sorry to email this late, but I need to know...can you do something for me in the next 20 minutes?

Time is up in just a few hours, and to stay in this race and win the Wisconsin primary, **Ted needs to raise another $213,264 before the Federal Election Commission fundraising deadline (more on that in Ted's email from earlier today).**

As the campaign's Director of Research and Analytics -- it's part of my job to keep on top of polling. You may have heard that Ted is surging to the front in Wisconsin, but what you don't know is this...

_**We MUST beat our FEC deadline goal to turn the polling surge in Wisconsin into a win.**_

That's where you come in -- we have ads running and a great team of volunteers on the ground -- [**but we need to raise (at bare minimum) $213,264 before midnight tomorrow night.**](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/34503322:sKC_mnCNy:m:1:1104269936:8A25EA73913AD273EC333CEDB928F375:r)

We're already cutting it too close -- [**so even if you've already given to help Ted in the last 48 hours, can you give again?**](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/34503322:sKC_mnCNy:m:1:1104269936:8A25EA73913AD273EC333CEDB928F375:r)

On Ted's behalf, I want to thank you for your dedication, prayers, and financial support.

It is keeping us going.

Thank you,

Chris Wilson
Director of Research and Analytics
Cruz for President

Chris, you must read this.

**FIRST:** The liberal elite media said we could never win, but we have won nine contests and are on the verge of securing the nomination.

**NEXT:** Donald Trump went on the attack, but we are now leading in multiple polls and neck-and-neck in key primary states like California.

**NOW:** There are only 48 hours left before the most important campaign finance deadline, and my campaign is coming up just short.

Chris, today is the day I need you by my side.

**In 48 hours, one of the biggest deadlines of my campaign is here.** Will you send immediate help before it gets here?

If you step up in the next few hours, I will have the resources to fight these attacks, win the nomination, and defeat Hillary Clinton.

name: Joe Sestak
subject: Mr. President, I've still got your 6
body:
[](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/1719bba/3476a20e/c78e0c8/2745fa26/3320482250/VEsH/ "http://joesestak.com")
---

The President and I share many of the same battle scars that have gained my deep respect for him. Therefore, in view of today's announcement of support for my opponent, I thought it important to highlight some of what we did in our service together for our nation, particularly when he first became Commander-in-Chief. In Congress, I needed to have the President's 6 (the military term for "I've got your back") because the nation needed him to have theirs:
* As a former Admiral, I wanted to ensure I had the President's 6 with my military expertise and credibility for the proper drawdown from the war in Iraq, despite opposition from some military leadership and leaders of the Republican Party.
* With my Harvard Ph.d in Political Economy, I wanted to have the President's 6 with the economic arguments to restore our economy through the Economic Stimulus bill, by saving the financial system and then placing safeguards on it, even though I represented a Republican congressional district.
* Because my then-four year old daughter survived brain cancer due to the military healthcare my nation provided my family, I brought with a passion that harrowing experience to having the President's 6 for the Affordable Care Act, so all Americans could have what I had to save my daughter - and I prized advancing Obamacare at town halls with the Tea Party and on FOX News.
* And on issues that had to do with our ideals - from repealing "Don't Ask, Don't Tell" to the closure of Gitmo to the DREAM Act - I stood steadfast in having the President's 6 both as a Congressman and today because he had our nation's.

I therefore have great respect for the President and the battles he and I fought together - for people - and we wear the same battle scars, with pride. But that is also why I have never asked the President - nor anyone else in a position of power - to have my 6, not even by asking for their endorsement. **As a leader, it is only about having _the people 's_ 6, and because I have theirs, they will have mine. May I ask you to join them?**

| [](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/1719bba/3476a20e/c78e0c8/2745fa25/3320482250/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gotyour6?refcode=16EM0330FBb1")
---
Warmly,
Joe

name: BernieSanders.com
subject: So much on the line in Wisconsin
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2016
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
Hillary Clinton left the campaign trail for yet another high-dollar fundraising event on Monday, but she shared some news about the state of the race that we agree with: she said the trail to the Democratic nomination "goes through Wisconsin."
We've won six of the last seven contests and have all the momentum. And we have a chance to build on that significantly when Wisconsin votes in a little over a week. It's a state Hillary Clinton once led by over 40 points, but we have cut the margin down to single digits.
One thing is for sure: we are fighting for every last vote. But with so much on the line, you can bet Hillary Clinton and the financial elite funding her campaign will be as well. That's why we have to ask:
[**Can Bernie count on you to contribute $3 before tomorrow's critical FEC fundraising deadline to help us win Wisconsin and the Democratic nomination?**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/why-wisconsin-matters?refcode=em160330s2-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/why-wisconsin-matters?refcode=em160330s2-full")
We have come so far in Wisconsin, and in this race, to come up short. We expect that it's going to be close, and every contribution made between now and tomorrow's deadline could make a real difference in determining the outcome. Thank you for continuing to stand with Bernie, and for leading this political revolution.
In solidarity,
Jeff Weaver
Campaign Manager
